ai_summary.one_liner: Three architectural patterns for building applications that adapt to Claude's evolving intelligence: use general tools, enable self-orchestration, and set boundaries carefully
ai_summary.problem: Agent frameworks encode assumptions about LLM limitations that become outdated as capabilities evolve, creating unnecessary bottlenecks on performance
ai_summary.solution: Three design patterns: (1) use general-purpose tools Claude already knows (bash, text editor), (2) let Claude self-orchestrate via code execution, progressive context, and memory persistence, (3) set boundaries through prompt caching, declarative tools, and security layers
ai_summary.key_result: Claude 3.5 Sonnet achieved 49% on SWE-bench Verified with just bash + text editor; Opus 4.6 BrowseComp improved from 45.3% to 61.6% with self-orchestration; Opus 4.6 reached 84% on BrowseComp with compaction
limitations: Patterns are prescriptive guidelines rather than empirically validated across diverse domains
SWE-bench and BrowseComp are specific benchmarks; generalization to other agent tasks is assumed but not shown
Cost-latency tradeoffs not quantified beyond prompt caching discount

research_gaps.question: 프롬프트 캐싱의 10% 비용 절감 외에 3가지 패턴 적용 시의 전체적인 비용-레이턴시 트레이드오프가 정량화되지 않아, 실제 프로덕션 환경에서의 경제성 판단이 어렵다
